---
title: ""
---

<!-- ── About ──────────────────────────────────────────────────── -->
::: {#about .site-section .hero-section}

:::: {.hero}

::: {.hero-photo}
![](images/photo.jpeg){.profile-photo}
:::

::: {.hero-text}
# Rosie Wood {.giorgio}

::: {.hero-role}
Research Software Engineer · Alan Turing Institute
:::

I am a Research Software Engineer and member of the Research Engineering Group (REG) and Research Computing team at the Alan Turing Institute.
I specialise in deploying software and models on GPU/HPC systems, developing open-source reproducible research software and applying computer vision and LLMs to real-world research problems.

::: {.hero-links}
<a href="https://github.com/rwood-97" class="btn btn-outline-primary btn-sm"><i class="bi bi-github me-1"></i>GitHub</a>
<a href="https://linkedin.com/in/rosie-w-01616715b" class="btn btn-outline-primary btn-sm"><i class="bi bi-linkedin me-1"></i>LinkedIn</a>
<a href="https://orcid.org/0000-0003-1623-1949" class="btn btn-outline-primary btn-sm"><i class="fa-brands fa-orcid me-1"></i>ORCID</a>
:::
:::

::::

:::

<!-- ── Projects ────────────────────────────────────────────────── -->
::: {#projects .site-section}

::: {.projects-header}
::: {.section-label}
Projects
:::
::: {.sort-controls}
<span class="toggle-label">Default</span>
<label class="toggle-switch">
  <input type="checkbox" id="sort-toggle">
  <span class="toggle-slider"></span>
</label>
<span class="toggle-label">Most Recent</span>
:::
:::

::: {#projects-list}

::: {.project-card data-year="2023" data-order="1"}
### [MapReader](https://github.com/maps-as-data/MapReader)
*2023–present*

Award-winning computer vision pipeline for large-scale analysis of historical maps.
:::

::: {.project-card data-year="2025" data-order="2"}
### [t0-1](https://github.com/alan-turing-institute/t0-1)
*2025–2026*

A RAG system which uses small language models fine-tuned for reasoning to answer domain specific queries (focused on NHS A-Z conditions).
:::

::: {.project-card data-year="2026" data-order="3"}
### [Eden](https://github.com/rwood-97/eden)
*2026–present*

A RAG system which uses small reasoning models to answer domain-specific queries about gardening (focused on RHS data).
:::

::: {.project-card data-year="2025" data-order="4"}
### [multi_node_inference](https://github.com/rwood-97/multi_node_inference)
*2025*

A series of scripts for running multi-node inference with LLMs across multiple nodes on HPC using vLLM (including containerised workflows).
:::

::: {.project-card data-year="2024" data-order="5"}
### [reginald](https://github.com/alan-turing-institute/reginald)
*2024*

A slack bot/chat interface which uses open source LLMs and RAG to answer queries about Turing internal data.
:::

:::

:::

<!-- ── CV ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── -->
<!-- ::: {#cv .site-section}

::: {.section-label}
CV
:::

Add CV content here

::: -->

<!-- ── Talks, Presentations & Recognition ───────────────────────── -->
::: {#talks .site-section}

::: {.section-label}
Talks & Presentations
:::

- ["Porting Microsoft's Aurora to Dawn"](https://www.cosec.ac.uk/calendar/annual-conference/2025-conference/) \[CoSeC/CIUK 2025, Manchester\]. *12/2025*
- [Isambard-AI Workshop](https://www.turing.ac.uk/events/brics-x-turing-isambard-ai-workshop) \[The Alan Turing Institute, London\]. *03/2026*
- Intro to HPC Workshop \[The Alan Turing Institute, London\]. *11/2025*
- [MapReader Autumn Data/Culture Workshop](https://www.turing.ac.uk/events/autumn-dataculture-workshop) \[The Alan Turing Institute, London\]. *10/2024*
- [MapReader Workshop](https://pretalx.com/spathum24/talk/FYVZZG/) \[Spatial Humanities 2024, Bamberg, Germany\]. *09/2024*
- [MapReader Summer Data/Culture Workshop](https://www.lancaster.ac.uk/events/the-dataculture-project-at-the-alan-turing-institute-is-offering-a-workshop-on-working-with-big-historical-data-from-maps-and-newspapers-in-lancaster-this-june/) \[Lancaster University\]. *06/2024*
- [MapReader Spring Data/Culture Workshop](https://www.turing.ac.uk/events/spring-dataculture-workshop-search-inside-maps-mapreader) \[The Alan Turing Institute, London\]. *04/2024*
- [MapReader Public Launch Event](https://livingwithmachines.ac.uk/event/mapreader-launch/) \[The Alan Turing Institute, London\]. *06/2023*
- Maps as Data Workshop \[BMA House, London\]. *03/2023*

::: {.section-label}
Recognition
:::

- Featured in SC25 HPC Ignites 'Women in HPC' series. *03/2025*
- MapReader awarded the 2023 Roy Rosenzweig Prize for Creativity in Digital History. *12/2023*

:::

<!-- ── Publications ─────────────────────────────────────────────── -->
::: {#publications .site-section}

::: {.section-label}
Publications
:::

{{< include _pubs_content.qmd >}}

:::

<script>
document.getElementById('sort-toggle').addEventListener('change', function () {
  const list = document.getElementById('projects-list');
  const cards = Array.from(list.querySelectorAll('.project-card'));

  cards.sort((a, b) => this.checked
    ? Number(b.dataset.year) - Number(a.dataset.year)
    : Number(a.dataset.order) - Number(b.dataset.order)
  );

  cards.forEach(card => list.appendChild(card));
});
</script>
